mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expansion and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising real retail sales indicates synchronized supply-demand expansion, which compresses credit spreads and lifts equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 2-year walk-forward test, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising consumer prices force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which increases borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered episodes, or the forward return differential between high and low inflation regimes fails to exceed 1.5% over a 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in new housing starts reflects sustained consumer confidence and credit availability, which supports construction-related earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 20-day Spearman correlation between HOUST momentum and SPX returns stays below 0.05 for three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index signals supply chain bottlenecks or capacity constraints, which erodes profit margins and triggers negative equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.0% over two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered contraction episodes, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.10 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment levels paired with accelerating real retail sales indicate broad-based income growth, which fuels consumption-driven earnings revisions and lifts equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a directional accuracy below 45% in out-of-sample testing, or the cross-sectional momentum of the joint signal fails to predict returns beyond a 10% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid GDP growth, elevated consumer credit balances amplify spending cycles, which boosts corporate revenues and generates positive equity returns over a one-month window.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in over 55% of high-growth triggered instances, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes in a multivariate framework.
